_comment: RAG v0 knobs (component 4). Variable-first: every retrieval/chunking/injection constant lives here WITH a rationale, never as a code literal. ayre_rag/config.py reads + validates these into a typed RagConfig; *_rationale siblings are ignored by the loader and exist only to document the WHY (document-tier-reasoning). Stack: pure Python sqlite3/FTS5 BM25 (no Rust/Burn, no Kiwix). See RAG_v0_Plan.md / RAG_v0_Build_Plan.md.
enabled_rationale: Guardrail 2 (additive, default-off): the whole RAG line is dead until this is flipped. Worst case Ayre ships exactly as stable as today with a dead flag. The UI's Settings toggle writes this (per-machine overlay at v1 UX; committed default stays false).
corpus_label_rationale: Human-facing name of the active corpus, shown in the RAG library section + stats. v0 = Simple English; at v0.5 this flips to 'English Wikipedia' alongside index_db_path (the pipeline is corpus-agnostic).
index_db_path_rationale: The FTS5 index db. Resolved relative to the app root (Project_Ayre/); '../../Ayre_Corpus/' lands beside the Wikipedia dump, OUTSIDE the app tree, and is gitignored (*.db) -- a large generated artifact must never be committed (consume-only; Distribution_Contents 'never-in-repo'). Absolute paths are honored as-is.
chunk_chars_rationale: Target chunk size (~300 tokens): a coherent grounding unit big enough to carry an idea, small enough that ~5 fit a modest injection budget. Paragraph-aligned (chunker never splits mid-paragraph unless a single paragraph exceeds this).
min_chunk_chars_rationale: A trailing remainder shorter than this merges into the previous chunk instead of becoming a runt chunk that dilutes ranking.
title_weight_rationale: bm25() column weight: a query term matching the article TITLE counts 5x a body match, so a query about 'Photosynthesis' surfaces that article's chunks over passing mentions elsewhere. Tunable in CP1 against the query CLI's scores.
retrieve_k_rationale: Candidate pool size: fetch the top 20 chunks by bm25 before applying the threshold + selecting inject_n. Wider than inject_n so the threshold/relative-trim have room to cut. Also the reranker window at v0.5.
inject_n_rationale: Max chunks actually injected into context (5 x 1200 chars is roughly 1500 tokens) after the threshold cut. Bounded again by context_fraction so a large context window can't be flooded.
score_threshold_rationale: ABSOLUTE floor on bm25(). SQLite returns more-negative = better and the raw score is corpus/query-relative, so no portable cutoff exists up front. Ships null = 'inject the top inject_n regardless' so retrieval is OBSERVABLE before tuning; CP1 calibrates a real value (e.g. -2.0) by eyeballing the query CLI's printed scores. Once set, hits with score > threshold are dropped; if nothing clears it, inject nothing.
score_rel_fraction_rationale: Relative trim, 0 = OFF (default). When >0, after the absolute floor keep hit i only if its score magnitude is within this fraction of the top hit's (|score_i| >= |top| * fraction) -- i.e. 'within X% of the best'. Included now (few lines) so CP2/v0.5 can suppress noisy tails without a rebuild.
context_fraction_rationale: Hard ceiling: injected RAG context never exceeds 25% of the model's context window, whatever inject_n says. Leaves room for the conversation + tools and coexists with the context meter (protect the budget the user can see).
per_article_char_cap_rationale: 0 = ingest full article text (Simple English articles are short, so full-text is cheap). >0 = truncate each article to N chars before chunking ('lead-only' mode) -- the enwiki hedge for full 7M-article coverage at a fraction of the index size/time if full-text ingest disappoints.
further_reading_rationale: When true, a 'English Wikipedia -- <title>' further-reading pointer is available for a grounded article (v0 cites Simple; enwiki is depth, clearly labeled, never the source -- citation honesty rule). It surfaces ONLY in the retrieved-context preview, never in the titles-only sources list. At v0.5 the enwiki pointer becomes the primary source once enwiki is the active corpus.
show_retrieved_context_rationale: Retrieved-context preview panel: default OFF, user-enablable in Settings. When on, a collapsible panel under a grounded reply shows the actual injected chunks (+ any further-reading pointer). The deduped titles-only sources list is ALWAYS shown regardless; this governs the raw-chunk preview only (chat-surface decision 4.3, transparency without clutter).
